subject: Public Health
title: Herd Immunity Explained
description: Learn Herd Immunity Explained in one minute.

hook.text: You don't need to vaccinate everyone to stop an epidemic—just enough people to break the chain.

buildup.text: Herd immunity occurs when enough people are immune that the pathogen can't find new hosts to infect.

discovery.text: The threshold varies by disease—measles needs 95% coverage, polio needs about 80%, flu needs around 50%.

twist.text: Herd immunity protects those who can't be vaccinated—newborns, immunocompromised patients, and the elderly.

climax.text: When vaccination rates drop below the threshold, outbreaks return—as measles resurgences have shown.

punchline.text: Vaccination isn't just personal protection. It's community armor.

quiz.question: What vaccination coverage does measles require for herd immunity?
